Figure 1: Overview of the DMP-MH framework. The proposed method operates through three sequential phases. Phase 1 (Private): Sensitivity-Bounded Graph Construction takes training pairs and computes a cosine similarity matrix, then clips node degrees to at most 𝐷max neighbors to bound local sensitivity and avoid hubness explosion. Phase 2 (Private): Differentially Private Motif Synthesis employs Noisy Mirror Descent with Gaussian noise N(0, 𝜎2I) added to the motif gradient, followed by rectified log-normalization to produce a sanitized synthetic graph ?̂? satisfying (𝜖, 𝛿)-Edge DP. The raw graph is then discarded as a privacy firewall. Phase 3 (Public): Dual-Stream Distillation Hashing trains an Image Net (ResNet-50 [16]) and Text Net (DistilBERT [29]) to reconstruct the topology of ?̂? via a holistic structural loss that enforces intra-modal and cross-modal alignment, with quantization regularization pushing embeddings toward binary codes. Query/test data Dte remain unseen during graph construction to ensure strict inductive evaluation.
